key_points: Van Rensselaer Hall has rooftop access and loyal alumni nostalgia
Off-campus landlords are a serious problem — multiple warned as predatory
Powelton Properties and PPG specifically named for deposit theft and poor repairs
key_quotes: DO NOT rent from Powelton Properties!!! They are dishonest, manipulative, and they lie.
I've successfully sued them for my deposit back. They will try every scare tactic under the sun.
I rented from them and they charged $250 for paint for three push pin holes.

key_points: Hard to make friends — co-op schedule fragments social cohesion constantly
Non-drinkers and non-party people feel genuinely isolated on campus
The 5-year timeline means watching friend groups graduate and disperse early
key_quotes: It just feels like there are zero opportunities to meet people at this school.
I don't drink and I'm not into frat parties or things like that.
All of my friends are going to graduate and it's kind of giving me FOMO.

key_points: On-campus Wawa closed, removing a key student food option
Vending machines regularly go empty with no explanation
DP Dough also reportedly closing — students feel food options shrinking
key_quotes: Bruh fr we already don't got shit.
You didn't hear it from me but we're losing DP Dough next week too.
Why walk all the way to a sterile dump that assumes you are a thief.

key_points: Co-op anxiety is severe — students reporting sleeplessness and constant fear
Nursing and CS students both describe mental breakdowns under workload
Chronically ill students report co-op system offers zero meaningful accommodation
key_quotes: This week has been so stressful that I can barely sleep without constant fear and worry.
I'm on a coop right now and I feel useless.
Being under a lot of stress has been causing me to think about relapsing.

key_points: Aid packages frequently fall far short of actual cost — families get blindsided
International students face $150k+ total cost even with scholarships
Seniors have nearly dropped out senior year due to unmet financial gaps
key_quotes: Is Drexel University worth a 150k student debt?
Congratulations, I hope your parents are rich or you're ready to work hard.
I'm a senior supporting myself through education with two quarters left that I can't afford.

key_points: Heavy reliance on adjunct professors — high turnover, inconsistent quality
10-week quarter system is brutal: fast-paced with little margin for error
Some professors are genuinely good but bad ones are disproportionately memorable
key_quotes: Drexel hires a ton of adjuncts so they pay less. I've had several professors who worked literally one semester only.
Not paying the good professors enough so they left, leaving the bad ones.
I know a lot of students aren't happy about moving to semesters instead of quarters.

key_points: Program advisors described as unhelpful and even mean without accountability
RA offers rescinded without explanation, citing vague 'operational changes'
Keynote speaker controversy at CCI graduation showed poor institutional judgment
key_quotes: Why do program advisors have no incentive to be helpful? Just trying to understand the mean behavior.
Yet another L for Drexel. For a university that makes an almost uncountable amount of bad choices.
Had my RA offer rescinded due to 'operational changes' — I don't even know what they mean.

key_points: Urban campus in West Philly — no traditional quad, buildings feel scattered
Neighborhood safety is a real concern: assaults, hit-and-runs, car thefts all reported
Philadelphia access is a genuine plus — great city if you can navigate it
key_quotes: So where is the campus exactly? It looks more like buildings scattered randomly around the town.
Saw another car on wheels stolen by Drexel Park. Must've been a raid last night.
It is still very much a concrete campus. Also there are tons of homeless people around.

key_points: Co-op is the school's strongest genuine asset — real companies, real salaries
CS and cybersecurity co-op market is now oversaturated — many students go multiple rounds without offers
Students who land co-ops at major employers often get full-time return offers
key_quotes: If you land a co-op at one of the bigger employers and they like you, you're pretty likely to get a job offer in your senior year.
Cybersecurity is very popular and the market is over saturated, even Drexel can't avoid these trends.
Co-op isn't even guaranteed — is it worth staying for?

key_points: High sticker price with aid that rarely covers actual need — a recurring complaint
Co-op salaries can offset living costs but don't come close to covering tuition debt
Low graduation rate (historically ~27%) raises serious ROI questions
key_quotes: Congrats. Be mindful of the debt this school will put you in and the post degree salary you'll be earning.
Brought up graduation rates (27%) at the admissions office and it made them rather defensive.
Is Drexel worth it for a CS degree because of the co-op? Co-ops are just as competitive as getting any other job.

key_points: Drexel is a grind school — co-op hustle replaces traditional college experience
Students who come for career outcomes tolerate the rest; social seekers often regret it
School spirit is low — alumni joke about distancing themselves from the Drexel name
key_quotes: I can't say I'm proud to be here either but this seems like a whole new level of shame.
MIP is tough... get ready to deal with the worst and best people alive at the same time.
Wearing my Drexel hoodie in DC, someone looked again and said 'o... Sorry' with a look of disappointment.

key_points: **Co-op**: CS/cybersecurity students going through D-round with zero interviews, severe market saturation
**Finances**: Students dropping out senior year, $150k+ debt for international students, aid gaps systemic
**Administration**: Advisor hostility, rescinded RA offers, questionable speaker choices, low graduation rate defensively ignored
key_quotes: I'm a senior with two quarters left that I can't afford by any means.
1st CS coop, zero interview requests. Feeling like I should've picked a different major at a different school.
For a university that makes an almost uncountable amount of bad choices, this is certainly one of them.

key_points: Wandering campus buildings on weekends reveals cool labs, nooks, and study spots
Student-built tools like OpenMario.com help navigate professor and course selection
Van Rensselaer Hall rooftop and strong alumni community within specific programs like Music Industry
key_quotes: Wandering buildings on weekends — so fun and interesting, so many cool spots I never considered could exist.
I had 10s of tabs wasting hours looking for the right professor — so I built OpenMario.
I have so many friends in the music industry program here and they absolutely love it.
